title: lichen_pubkey_to_iid and lichen_eui64_to_iid U/L bit handling differs without explanation
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c

- lichen_eui64_to_iid (lines 46-57): XORs U/L bit (iid[0] ^= 0x02)
- lichen_pubkey_to_iid (lines 59-104): Clears U/L bit (iid[0] &= ~0x02)

Risk: The two functions handle the U/L bit differently:
1. eui64_to_iid: Flips the bit (per RFC 4291 for MAC-derived IIDs)
2. pubkey_to_iid: Clears the bit (marks as locally-administered)

The comments explain this but a contributor may not understand WHY they differ. Both approaches are semantically correct but for different reasons:
- EUI-64 from MAC: Flip U/L because MAC universal bit has opposite meaning from IID
- Pubkey-derived: Clear U/L because it is not a globally-unique address

Pattern contributors may misunderstand: Copying one function style when writing the other, or not understanding why the bit manipulation differs.

Suggested fix: Add a block comment at the start of the file explaining U/L bit semantics across all IID derivation methods in this module.
